summary:
The available evidence does not indicate that midwives’ competence is diminished when working in rural areas; their performance remains comparable across settings based on established standards for maternal-fetal care outcomes. Competence appears unaffected by geography because all referenced cases emphasize uniformity in assessing risks and making decisions irrespective of location. 

(Note: This response assumes the original query refers broadly to "competency" rather than specific skills such as access to resources.)

(End of Round)
